skill.name: asset-creator
skill.description: Generate project-ready visual assets with an OpenAI DALL-E helper script and save them into public asset folders.
skill.summary: Generate SaaS-style images and illustrations through a reusable OpenAI image script.
skill.author: AayushBaniya2006

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby-style backtick execution in Markdown are false positives caused by inline code and fenced command examples. The helper script legitimately reads OPENAI_API_KEY, calls OpenAI image generation, decodes base64 image data, and writes an output file, but output path inputs are not restricted to the intended public directory.

medium_findings.title: Unrestricted Output Path Components
medium_findings.description: The script accepts folder and filename arguments and joins them into a filesystem path before writing image data. It does not restrict traversal segments or verify that the final path remains under the intended public directory.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The validation schema only checks that folder and filename are strings. The later path.join and writeFileSync calls use those values without containment checks.

medium_findings.title: External Image Generation Sends Prompts to OpenAI
medium_findings.description: The helper uses the OpenAI SDK to generate images from the supplied prompt. This is expected behavior for the skill, but users should avoid placing secrets or confidential project data in prompts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The OpenAI client is initialized with OPENAI_API_KEY and calls images.generate with the user prompt. This confirms network data sharing, but it matches the stated purpose.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Many external command alerts point to inline code formatting, fenced Bash examples, or documentation references. They describe how to run the helper script and do not show hidden command execution by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation and command examples. No Ruby backtick operator or shell execution API is present in those files.

low_findings.title: API Key Access Is Required but Sensitive
low_findings.description: The script checks OPENAI_API_KEY and passes it to the OpenAI client. This is necessary for image generation, but the credential must be managed through the environment and not committed to files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The key is read from process.env and used as SDK configuration. I did not find evidence that the key is logged or written to disk.

low_findings.title: Base64 Decode Is Expected Image Handling
low_findings.description: The script requests base64 image output from OpenAI, decodes it into a buffer, and writes the image file. This is normal for the selected response format and is not obfuscation by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The base64 value comes directly from the OpenAI image response. Its use is tied to image file creation, not concealed code or command payloads.

dangerous_patterns.title: Path Join With User-Controlled Folder and Filename
dangerous_patterns.description: The folder and filename arguments influence the output path. The script should normalize and enforce that the resolved path stays inside the public directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code validates presence but not path safety. A containment check is not visible near the file write.

content.value_statement: Teams need fast visual assets that match a product style. This skill creates DALL-E prompts, runs a helper script, and saves images into project asset folders.
actual_capabilities: Generates detailed prompts for minimalist SaaS images and illustrations.
Runs a TypeScript helper script with prompt, filename, folder, and style arguments.
Uses OpenAI DALL-E 3 through the OpenAI Node SDK.
Saves generated PNG files into public asset subfolders.
Supports vivid and natural image styles.
Provides React or MDX image usage guidance after creation.
limitations: Requires OPENAI_API_KEY in the local environment.
Needs Node, npx, tsx, and package dependencies to be available.
Does not validate output paths against traversal or absolute path input.
DALL-E may not return true transparent backgrounds.

use_cases.title: Create Marketing Hero Art
use_cases.description: Generate a polished hero image for a landing page and save it under a marketing assets folder.

use_cases.title: Produce App Illustration Assets
use_cases.description: Create consistent illustrations for onboarding, empty states, or feature pages in a SaaS application.

use_cases.title: Prototype Visual Direction
use_cases.description: Test multiple prompt ideas and style settings before final design production.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Asset Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Create one simple image for a product page.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a minimalist SaaS illustration of [subject]. Save it as [filename] in assets/images using vivid style.

prompt_templates.title: Marketing Image Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Generate a landing page visual with a specific folder.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a hero image for [campaign or feature]. Use a clean flat style and save it as [filename] in assets/marketing.

prompt_templates.title: Style Controlled Asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Match a calmer visual tone for product UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image of [subject] with modern SaaS composition, white background, and natural style. Save it as [filename] in [folder].

prompt_templates.title: Asset Set Planning
prompt_templates.scenario: Create several related assets with consistent naming.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan and generate three related illustrations for [workflow]. Use consistent composition, clear filenames, and separate public asset folders where useful.

output_examples.input: Create a hero image showing an analytics dashboard on a laptop.
output_examples.output: A detailed DALL-E prompt is prepared, the image helper runs with a marketing folder, and a PNG is saved for site usage.

output_examples.input: Generate an onboarding empty-state illustration for team invitations.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a minimalist invitation-themed image, stores it in the public assets area, and provides a React image path.

output_examples.input: Make a natural-style feature image for automated reports.
output_examples.output: The helper runs with the natural style option and produces a named PNG in the requested public subfolder.

best_practices: Use safe folder names under public assets and avoid path traversal characters.
Keep prompts specific about subject, composition, background, and style.
Store API keys in environment variables and keep them out of project files.
anti_patterns: Do not include secrets, customer data, or private source details in image prompts.
Do not pass absolute paths or parent directory segments as folder names.
Do not assume generated images have transparent backgrounds without checking the file.

faq.question: What image model does this skill use?
faq.answer: The helper script uses DALL-E 3 through the OpenAI Node SDK.

faq.question: Which environment variable is required?
faq.answer: OPENAI_API_KEY must be set before running the helper script.

faq.question: Where are generated images saved?
faq.answer: Images are saved under public plus the folder argument, with assets/images as the default folder.

faq.question: Can it create transparent PNG files?
faq.answer: It requests helpful backgrounds, but DALL-E output may still need post-processing for true transparency.

faq.question: Is this safe for confidential prompts?
faq.answer: No. Prompts are sent to OpenAI, so confidential or regulated information should not be included.

faq.question: What should be improved before broad deployment?
faq.answer: The script should restrict folder and filename values to safe paths inside the intended public directory.
